section.title: Translation Memory Store
section.para: An XLIFF document conforms to this document as a Translation Memory Store if:

listitem.para: It is a conformant [XLIFF-2.3-part2] (or later) document.

Every stored translation memory entry is represented as an XLIFF <unit> containing one <segment> per entry, with <source> and, where a translation is available, <target> content, and no lossy conversion of inline content has been applied to represent that entry.
Where provenance information is recorded for an entry, it is expressed using the XLIFF 2.3 Provenance module rather than free-text notes or properties.

section.title: Translation Memory Exchange Package
section.para: A ZIP archive conforms to this document as a Translation Memory Exchange Package if:

listitem.para: It contains an xliff-manifest.xml file at its root, as described in Manifest File.
Every XLIFF file listed in the manifest is present in the archive and conforms to this document as a Translation Memory Store.

Every XLIFF file listed in the manifest declares a single srcLang, matching the srcLang of the aligned set that contains it, and a single trgLang, matching the trgLang recorded for that file in the manifest.

For every aligned set in the manifest with more than one member, corresponding translation units across the member files use the same <unit> id value (and, where segmented, the same <segment> id value), as described in Package Structure.
Where a <file> entry in the manifest carries a hash attribute, the content of the file it references matches that hash.
